seoTitle: Stake Hill vs Stirling Estate — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stake Hill and Stirling Estate. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stake Hill vs Stirling Estate: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stake Hill and Stirling Estate.
heading: Stake Hill vs Stirling Estate — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stake Hill, Western Australia and Stirling Estate, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stake Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Stirling Estate (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stake Hill or Stirling Estate safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stake Hill has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Stirling Estate scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stake Hill scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Stirling Estate scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stake Hill or Stirling Estate?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stake Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
